data.title: As a quality engineer, I can verify end-to-end that relates_to drift detection and link suggestion work together
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create an end-to-end test scenario that verifies the full workflow: two epics linked via relates_to, one closes, and the next sprint drift check on the other epic emits REPLAN_TRIGGER. Also verifies the brainstorm suggestion path that creates relates_to links.
**Why**: Unit tests for individual components (S1 drift detection, S2 link suggestion) verify each piece in isolation. This test scenario verifies the integration between relates_to link creation, epic closure detection, and REPLAN_TRIGGER emission in a realistic multi-epic workflow.
**Scope**:
- IN: Test scenario with two epics linked via relates_to, epic closure triggering drift detection, REPLAN_TRIGGER emission verification, test for brainstorm suggestion creating the link that drift detection later follows
- OUT: Performance testing, stress testing, testing with more than two linked epics

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a test scenario verifies that when two epics are linked via relates_to and one closes, the next sprint drift check on the other epic emits REPLAN_TRIGGER
  ← Satisfies: "After deployment, if two epics are linked via relates_to and one closes, the next sprint drift check on the other epic's stories emits REPLAN_TRIGGER — verified by test scenario with two linked epics"
- When this story is complete, the test scenario covers both the drift detection path and the brainstorm suggestion path
  ← Satisfies: combined verification of SC1-SC5
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Test should use the ticket CLI to create ephemeral test epics rather than relying on production ticket state
- [Testing] REPLAN_TRIGGER output parsing should use the canonical parsing prefix from the replan-observability contract
- [Reliability] Test cleanup should remove test epics and links after verification
